stories.headline: Iran warns US Navy against entering Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil surrenders some gains after Washington denies claim that Tehran had struck an American warship

stories.headline: Iran warns US to stay out of Hormuz after Trump says US will ‘guide’ ships
stories.summary: President Trump says the US will begin 'Project Freedom' on Monday to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: War preparations under way in Iran as Hormuz tensions with US escalate
stories.summary: Amid stalled talks and sparring over key waterway, Iranian media say US warship struck, but Washington denies incident.

stories.headline: Iran war: What’s happening on day 66 as Trump announces Hormuz mission?
stories.summary: Trump orders new Hormuz mission after Iran says it receives US response to its peace proposal.

stories.headline: Japan PM says Iran war oil crisis having ‘enormous impact’ in Asia Pacific
stories.summary: Sanae Takaichi makes comments during a visit to Australia, where she signs agreements on energy supplies.

stories.headline: Five people killed in Russian missile attack on Ukraine’s Kharkiv region
stories.summary: Local governor says several injured in the attack with at least 10 houses struck by Russian missiles.

stories.headline: Trump Says U.S. Will Help Stranded Ships Leave Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Trump offered few details on how the new program, which he called Project Freedom, would work, but said interference would be dealt with “forcefully.”

stories.headline: UAE Issues Missile Warning as Rising Tensions Risk US-Iran Truce
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates on Monday issued a missile threat warning for the first time since a ceasefire between the US and Iran took hold about a month ago, underscoring the fragility of diplomatic efforts to end a war that has killed thousands and disrupted global energy supplies.

stories.headline: Iran threatens retaliation after Trump says U.S. will guide ships in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The president left unclear what measures the U.S. would take and whether any forces would be at risk. Iran said it would strike any foreign force approaching the strait.

stories.headline: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
stories.summary: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

stories.headline: US launches ‘Project Freedom’ to escort ships through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Trump announced the US would begin guiding ships on Monday through the strategic strait, a major transport route for oil, gas and fertiliser.

stories.headline: Israeli attacks kill dozens within days in Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.summary: The health ministry says 77 people have been killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon since Thursday.

stories.headline: Armenia hosts major EU summits in pivot away from Russia
stories.summary: European leaders gathered in Armenia for two major summits, as security, trade, and the war in Ukraine dominate talks.

stories.headline: Will Hormuz crisis spur Asia to boost oil reserve cooperation?
EU and US trade chiefs to meet as tariff tensions escalate
stories.summary: The Turnberry deal is still under negotiation between EU governments and lawmakers before it can take effect on the EU side, and the Commission warned that if the US takes actions deemed inconsistent with the agreement, it reserves the right to consider all options.

stories.headline: Bolivia Plots Global Bond Market Return After Four-Year Hiatus
stories.summary: Bolivia is gauging investor interest for its first dollar bond sale in four years after the new market-friendly government avoided a default on external debt payments in March.

stories.headline: Palantir Looks to Prove It Doesn’t Belong in Software Selloff
stories.summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. heads into its first-quarter earnings with something to prove to investors: That it’s been wrongly caught up in the selloff that has gripped software stocks for months.

stories.headline: Modi’s party poised for big wins in Indian state elections
stories.summary: The ruling BJP's lead in two of four key states is expected to significantly weaken the opposition.

stories.headline: ASEAN states shift oil imports toward Brunei, Libya and US
Roberta Metsola clashes with Azerbaijan's Aliyev at European summit in Armenia
stories.summary: Aliyev lashed out at the European Parliament over its "obsession" with the country, announcing a suspension of cooperation. Metsola offered a sharp rebuttal in defense of her institution: "Won't change the way we work."

stories.headline: Celebrations as Modi’s ruling party poised to take control of India's West Bengal state
stories.summary: Supporters of Indian prime minister Narendra Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) celebrate as trends released by the Election Commission show BJP leading in 176 out of 294 seats in West Bengal.

stories.headline: Antisemitism 'allowed to come into the open' says Bondi victim's daughter
stories.summary: Sheina Gutnick was the first person to give evidence at the royal commission investigating the attack.

stories.headline: Nigeria plans to repatriate nationals willing to leave South Africa after attacks
stories.summary: There has been a wave of anti-migrant protests in South Africa, some of which have turned violent.

stories.headline: Samsung family pays off record $8bn inheritance tax bill
stories.summary: The bill is tied to the estate left by the firm's late chairman Lee Kun-hee, who died in  October 2020.

stories.headline: Blackstone and Goldman among backers for $1.5bn JV with Anthropic
stories.summary: New consulting company to advise Wall Street groups on how to deploy its AI across their investment portfolios

stories.headline: ECB Likely to Hike Rates with Capital Economics' McKeown
stories.summary: Jennifer McKeown, chief global economist at Capital Economics, discussed the near-term outlook for central bank policy, highlighting that the European Central Bank appears more likely to raise interest rates soon compared to the Bank of England or the Federal Reserve. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Spirit Airlines Seeks to Start Orderly Wind-Down to Sell Assets
stories.summary: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. is set to start an orderly wind-down process to sell its assets, capping the downfall of the low-cost carrier that has filed for bankruptcy twice in recent years and failed to clinch a last-minute US government rescue.

stories.headline: Portugal Tightens Citizenship Rules After Immigration Spike
stories.summary: With Portugal’s foreign population reaching unprecedented levels, the country’s president has signed into law a bill that will double the amount of time needed for foreigners to obtain citizenship.

stories.headline: FedEx, UPS Shares Tumble on Amazon’s ‘Watershed’ Logistics Move
stories.summary: US transportation stocks plunged Monday morning after Amazon.com Inc. announced expanded logistics offerings that will turn it into a major competitor for parcel carriers and air freight companies, and also impact truckers and third-party brokers.

stories.headline: UAE Says Swap Talks With US Are About Joining ‘Elite’ Group
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates signaled its currency swap talks with the US were about joining a small club with access to Federal Reserve liquidity lines rather than a need for external financing.

stories.headline: EU Power Markets Lowers Hourly Price Floor to -600 Euros
stories.summary: European power markets are set to cut the hourly price limit to -€600 per megawatt-hour after an intense period of volatility where the existing floor price was reached a number of times.

stories.headline: These Are the Players in the Million-Dollar Dinosaur Fossil Business
stories.summary:
Why are dinosaur bones joining the collections of millionaires and billionaires like Ken Griffin?

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, we are joined by Salomon Aaron, a director at London-based gallery David Aaron. He tells Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway how the private market for fossils actually works and how similar it is to the market for art and other antiquities. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Surprise Rate Hold Upends Colombia’s Markets Weeks Before Vote
stories.summary: Colombia’s peso and short-term rates plunged on Monday following the central bank’s unexpected decision to hold borrowing costs just weeks before a pivotal election.

stories.headline: Trump-Linked World Liberty Sues Justin Sun for Defamation
stories.summary: World Liberty Financial said it is suing Justin Sun for defamation, accusing the billionaire crypto entrepreneur of running a coordinated media campaign against the Trump-linked project after he filed his own lawsuit against it last month.

stories.headline: Germany’s Klingbeil Renews Call for EU Windfall Tax on Energy
stories.summary: German Vice Chancellor Lars Klingbeil continued to push for a European Union windfall tax to cushion the fallout from the energy price spike for households and companies.

stories.headline: India loses its last left-wing government after five decades
stories.summary: Kerala in India, which gave the world its first democratically elected communist government, has voted the left out.

stories.headline: Formula One plans return to V8 engines as electrical revolution loses spark
stories.summary: F1 cars could return to traditional V8 engines by 2030, FIA President Mohammed Ben Sulayem says at Miami GP.

stories.headline: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
stories.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

stories.headline: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.summary: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.summary: Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”.Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May

stories.headline: Oil tanker hijacked off coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia
stories.summary: It is the second time an oil tanker has been hijacked in the area in the past 10 days.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
stories.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

stories.headline: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.headline: Trump says he will hike tariffs on EU cars to 25%

stories.headline: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.headline: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?

stories.summary: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists

stories.headline: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.
